rulings.Case Title: MALCOLM MEYERS VS. CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Deft Roebbelen'S For Interrogatories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 20, 2012, LINE 1, DEFENDANT ROEBBELEN CONTRACTING, INC. Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant Roebbelen'S For Interrogatories, Set One. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No tentative; parties to appear. (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DELASHON MONIQUE GREEN, SURVIVING SPOUSE OF et al VS. D.H.S. GLOBAL INVESTMENTS LLC, DOING BUSINESS AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notcie Of Motion And Motion For Relief Pursuant To Ccp 473(B)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 20, 2012, Line 9, PLAINTIFF DELASHON MONIQUE GREEN, , SARIYAH FULBRIGHT BY AND THROUGH HER GUARDIAN, ANA'STAJA FULBRIGHT BY AND THROUGH, TE'JANEY SCOTT, BY AND THROUGH's Motion For Relief Pursuant To Ccp 473(B). Granted. Plaintiffs' failure to oppose and appear at the hearing on October 9, 2012 is due to the surprise of Plaintiffs'current counsel Mr. Cartwright. Former counsel Mr. Konstantin acted with inexcusable neglect in his handling of the discovery that led to the issuance of the Court's order on October 9, 2012. The court grants discretionary relief per CCP 473(b) to Plaintiffs and their counsel as to the discovery and monetary sanctions issued by Court order of October 9, 2012. In connection with this motion, monetary sanctions of $5,187.00 against Mr. Konstantin are warranted for Defendants' attorney fees incurred by the ex parte application for order shortening time and the instant motion. Mr. Konstantin shall pay $ 5,187.00 to Defendant Terrance Alan no later than February 22, 2013. If they have not already done so, Plaintiffs shall serve verified discovery responses to the discovery identified in the October 9, 2012 order, without objection, no later than December 31, 2012 at 4:00 pm. Other than as set forth herein, this Order does not affect any other sanctions order previously imposed on Plaintiffs and Mr. Konstantin. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MING ZHU, LLC VS. SAN FRANCISCO MEDICAL IMAGING, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Ming Zhu'S Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 20, 2012, LINE 7, Plaintiff Ming Zhu'S Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in full, including $885.00 in sanctions, for reasons set forth in moving party¿s papers; defendants Ruvin Feygenberg and San Francisco Medical Imaging, Inc. do not oppose the motion and have not provided any response to the requests for admission. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES J NGUYEN et al VS. ANTHONY NGUYEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs James Nguyen And Madison Nguyen'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demand
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 20, 2012, LINE 7, Plaintiffs James Nguyen And Madison Nguyen'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demand. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No tentative; parties to appear. (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: QUALITY FRESH PRODUCE, INC. VS. AMIN ABU-GOSH, IND. & DBA SWEET JOANNA'S CAFE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 20, 2012, Line 19, PLAINTIFF QUALITY FRESH PRODUCE, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and is in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MXB HOLDINGS, LP A DELAWARE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al VS. ADAM LEVIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Pltfs Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 20, 2012, LINE 5, PLAINTIFF MXB HOLDINGS, LP A DELAWARE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, MICHAEL JACKSON, HECKLER CONSULTANCY, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY, SV ANGEL, LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY Motion To Compel The Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in full, including $747.50 in sanctions, for reasons set forth in moving party¿s papers; defendants Adam Levin and Tristen Lazareff do not oppose the motion and have not provided any response to the document request. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (302/JPT)
